response.justification: Candidate has strong full-stack and distributed-systems exposure (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of req/s, Optilyz pricing platform) and current hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, which aligns with the agentic-development mindset. However, the resume is light on demonstrated Python depth (skills list omits Python entirely) and lacks explicit Staff-level technical-strategy/stakeholder-coordination evidence despite the recent title. Worth a screen to probe Python proficiency, scale of distributed-systems ownership, and strategic scope.
strengths: Real high-throughput distributed-systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/s)
Current hands-on work with AI agents, MCP code generators, and agentic workflows at re:cinq
Strong software-craftsmanship background — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based dev, teaching/workshops
concerns: Python is not listed in skills; role requires strong Python
Limited explicit evidence of Staff-level technical strategy, stakeholder management, and on-call/incident leadership
Heavy DLang/Node.js focus historically; depth in modern distributed Python systems unclear
